clues: Highly selective, this medication inhibits DNA-dependent RNA polymerase, stopping RNA synthesis in bacterial cells. It is also a potent inducer of liver enzymes such as CYP450
The name of this medication was coined by developers after seeing a French film, Rififi, where the storyline was felt to mirror how this antibiotic disrupts bacterial cells
Because it is a naturally vibrant reddish-orange compount, it can deeply stain bodily fluid such as tears, sweat, and urine
In the mid to late 19th century, this disease was thought to be a severe infection, often misdiagnosed as pseudotrichinosis (a parasitic infection) because of the severe muscle swelling and weakness
Strongly associated with underlying malignancies, medical guidelines recommend screening adult patients for hidden cancers within the first two years of diagnosis
Associated with often symmetrical rashes on sun-exposed areas and proximal weakness, the skin findings are highly specific and virtually pathognomonic
Previously physicians attempted to treat this with surgical excision, but this was ineffective. This disease is now managed with immunosuppressants
This is associated with underlying systemic diseases in over 50% of cases such as inflammatory bowel diseases, rheumatoid arthritis, and leukemia
An inflammatory skin disease, some may have leared the 5 P's: painful, progressive, purple, pretibial, and pathergy

In the 3rd century BCE, Hippocrates based his medical theories on four 'humors', believed to govern a person's temperment and physical health. This substance was believed to be associated with individuals who are calm, unemotional, and stoic
When healthy, your body produces about 1 liter of this every day
Primarily made of water, this substance helps coat your airways and digestive tract and can trap dust, pollen, and harmful pathogens
Early attempts to isolate this in standard cell cultures were unsuccessful because it only replicates effectively at cooler tempratures of about 33C rather than standard body temperature of 37C
In the same family as poliovirus, there are more than 160 distinct strains or serotypes of this virus
Named after the Greek word for nose, this is the most common cause of the common cold and responsible for up to 50% of all cases
